title: Desenvolvimento Dynamics Sênior
descriptionHtml:
O Itaú é a marca mais valiosa do Brasil, a melhor empresa para trabalhar segundo o Great Place to Work e o maior banco da América Latina. Nós impactamos diariamente mais de 98 milhões de clientes em 18 países.

Somos feitos de pessoas e acreditamos que ter um time com pluralidade de origens, culturas, crenças, experiências, raças, deficiências, gêneros, orientações afetivo-sexuais e gerações ampliam as perspectivas e contribuem para um clima de respeito e valorização das diferenças. O COE (Centro de Excelência) Dynamics do Itaú está em busca um profissional de desenvolvimento Dynamics Sênior para integrar nossa equipe de TI. Você será uma referência técnica no time, atuando em projetos de alta complexidade baseados em Microsoft Dynamics 365 e Power Platform, além de mentorar desenvolvedores menos experientes.

Estamos buscando uma pessoa para a posição de profissional Dynamics, que queira se desenvolver e aprender, compartilhar, colaborar e inovar, entregando valor para todos os nossos clientes.

Nessa Função Você Vai

  • Liderar tecnicamente o desenvolvimento de soluções complexas no Dynamics 365
  • Atuar como mentor e referência técnica para o time
  • Desenvolver e otimizar Plugins, Custom APIs e Virtual Entities
  • Criar WebResources e componentes PCF (Power Apps Component Framework)
  • Definir e implementar soluções arquiteturais baseadas em padrões de projeto
  • Construir e manter esteiras de CI/CDpara deploy de Solutions
  • Criar Custom Connectors e gerenciar políticas deDLP no Power Automate
  • Analisar Pull Requests e realizar code reviews
  • Comunicar-se com outras equipes, compartilhando aprendizados e boas práticas

Conhecimento Funcional

  • Conhece limitações das parametrizações funcionais e identifica quando é necessária customização
  • Domínio em edição de Ribbon (PowerFx e Ribbon Workbench)

Linguagem de Programação – C#

  • Conhecimento avançado em C#
  • Domínio do SDK do Dynamics
  • Experiência sólida com testes unitários, mocks e testes de integração

Front-end

  • Conhecimento avançado em JavaScript
  • Criação de WebResources e/ou PCF
  • Domínio de frameworks: jQuery, Angular, React
  • Conhecimento avançado em SDK e WebAPI do Dynamics

Back-end

  • Domínio em Plugins
  • Criação de Virtual Entities
  • Otimização de performance
  • Capacidade de identificar pontos que performam ou degradam a aplicação
  • Domínio de Custom APIs, Pré e Post Images, Async e Sync

Cloud / Arquitetura

  • Conhecimento em soluções baseadas em microsserviços e eventos
  • Domínio de DDD, SOLID, MVC e Design Patterns
  • Conhecimento avançado em .NET 8 ou superior
  • Familiaridade com cofres e gestão de segredos
  • Tecnologias: Kafka, Service Bus, Event Hub, API Gateway, Kubernetes
  • Protocolos de comunicação
  • Experiência com Cloud (Azure e/ou AWS)

Solutions

  • Conhecimento avançado em Dynamics Solutions
  • Capacidade de resolver problemas complexos em Solutions
  • Uso de Patches
  • Experiência em esteiras de CI/CD

Controle de Versão (Git)

  • Análise de PRs e realização de code reviews
  • Resolução avançada de conflitos
  • Gerenciamento de repositórios (acessos, documentação e branches)
  • Integração de controle de versão com Canvas Apps

Power Automate

  • Criação de Custom Connectors
  • Entendimento de restrições de DLP (Data Loss Prevention)
  • Domínio em flows complexos com integrações

Power Apps (Canvas / Custom Pages)

  • Domínio da linguagem PowerFx
  • Conhecimento dos prós e contras entre Model Driven, Canvas e Custom Pages

Ao se tornar um ituber você tem_

  • Vale-Transporte
  • Vale-Refeição (Restaurantes) / Vale-Alimentação (Supermercados)
  • Plano médico (Fundação Saúde Itaú ou Central Nacional Unimed)
  • Plano odontológico (Odontoprev ou Interodonto)
  • Seguro de Vida
  • PLR – Participação nos Lucros e Resultados (Mediante resultados do banco)
  • Previdência privada
  • Descontos exclusivos em nossos produtos financeiros
  • Licença maternidade e paternidade estendida
  • Auxílio Creche / Babá (para papais e mamães)
  • Incentivo a estudos
  • Wellhub ou TotalPass
  • Acesso aos Clubes de Lazer Itaú (Guarapiranga / Itanhaém e São Sebastião)
  • + Algumas vantagens que você pode conhecer durante o processo 😊

Conheça mais sobre nossa cultura: https://www.itau.com.br/relacoes-com-investidores/noticias/cultura-itubers-e-nosso-novo-conjunto-de-valores/

  • Estimulamos candidaturas de pessoas com deficiência em todas as nossas posições, sendo assim, sinta-se à vontade para se candidatar em vagas sinalizadas como exclusivas ou não para pessoas com deficiência. Somos comprometidos com a inclusão e valorizamos a diversidade.

title: Engenharia de Software Frontend Angular Sr | Agro Varejo
descriptionHtml:
Sobre o Itaú Unibanco

O Itaú é a marca mais valiosa do Brasil, a melhor empresa para trabalhar segundo o Great Place to Work e o maior banco da América Latina. Nós impactamos diariamente mais de 98 milhões de clientes em 18 países.

Somos feitos de pessoas e acreditamos que ter um time com pluralidade de origens, culturas, crenças, experiências, raças, deficiências, gêneros, orientações afetivo-sexuais e gerações ampliam as perspectivas e contribuem para um clima de respeito e valorização das diferenças.

Modelo de Trabalho - Híbrido Sem Escala (8x Presenciais por mês - Jabaquara/SP).

Buscamos pessoas que querem transformar e crescer em equipe, criar e desenvolver uma nova plataforma com os melhores conceitos de arquitetura, engenharia e operações. Somos um time que cuida da jornada de parcerias do cliente Agro.

O grande desafio aqui, é construir uma plataforma, para controlar o fluxo de Fiscalização do Agro Varejo. Um time engajado, com autonomia e garra para construir produtos digitais no Itaú, vivenciando uma cultura e modelo diferenciados dentro do melhor banco do mundo!

Como Será o Dia a Dia Da Pessoa

  • Criação, Desenvolvimento e evolução nas customizações para o Agro Varejo;
  • Desenho e definição das peças a serem utilizadas na plataforma;
  • Desenvolvimento em Angular, IDS;
  • Uso eficiente dos recursos ofertados pela AWS;
  • Participar de sessões de code review, indicando pontos de melhoria e otimização dos codigos;
  • Participará desde o design da jornada;
  • Participar de inceptions, discoverys e backlog do squad;
  • Atuação como referência técnica dentro do time;
  • Apoiar no run do squad.

No que você precisa mandar bem?

  • Experiência sólida com Angular;
  • Conhecimento em microfrontends;
  • Atuação em desenvolvimento de jornadas para o cliente final.

DIFERENCIAIS

  • Conhecimento em UX/UI;
  • Experiência prévia com AWS;
  • Vivência em ambientes com forte integração entre front-end e negócio.

Ao se tornar um ituber você tem_

  • Vale-Transporte
  • Vale-Refeição (Restaurantes) / Vale-Alimentação (Supermercados)
  • Plano médico (Fundação Saúde Itaú ou Central Nacional Unimed)
  • Plano odontológico (Odontoprev ou Interodonto)
  • Seguro de Vida
  • PLR – Participação nos Lucros e Resultados (Mediante resultados do banco)
  • Previdência privada
  • Descontos exclusivos em nossos produtos financeiros
  • Licença maternidade e paternidade estendida
  • Auxílio Creche / Babá (para papais e mamães)
  • Incentivo a estudos
  • Wellhub ou TotalPass
  • Acesso aos Clubes de Lazer Itaú (Guarapiranga / Itanhaém e São Sebastião)
  • + Algumas vantagens que você pode conhecer durante o processo 😊

Conheça mais sobre nossa cultura: https://www.itau.com.br/relacoes-com-investidores/noticias/cultura-itubers-e-nosso-novo-conjunto-de-valores/

  • Estimulamos candidaturas de pessoas com deficiência em todas as nossas posições, sendo assim, sinta-se à vontade para se candidatar em vagas sinalizadas como exclusivas ou não para pessoas com deficiência. Somos comprometidos com a inclusão e valorizamos a diversidade.

title: Senior Software Engineer
descriptionHtml:
• About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 122 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we're building.

Listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: NU), we combine proprietary technology, data intelligence, and an efficient operating model to deliver financial products that are simple, accessible, and human.

Our impact has been recognized by global rankings such as Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies, and Forbes World’s Best Bank. Visit our institutional page https://international.nubank.com.br/careers/

Engineering at Nubank

We strive for state-of-the-art software development practices, that currently includes a variety of technologies. While we value candidates that are familiar with them, we are also confident that software engineers who are interested in joining Nubank will be able to learn from our team.

  • Horizontally scalable microservices written mostly in Clojure, using Finagle and leveraging upon functional programming techniques and hexagonal architecture
  • High throughput jobs and inter-service communication using Kafka
  • Continuous Integration and Deployment into AWS
  • Storing data in Datomic and DynamoDB
  • Monitoring and observability with Prometheus
  • Running as much as possible in Kubernetes

We are a process-light organization that values human interactions. We value working in small, independent teams that feel like small startups within the company, and eschew coupling and centralizing where realistic. We understand that execution is the key to success and optimize processes to remove bottlenecks.

As a Software Engineer, You’re Expected To

  • Work with large scale distributed systems
  • Collaborate with building microservices
  • Have practical knowledge of agile software development methodologies
  • Work with continuous delivery and infrastructure as code

Our Benefits

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/ Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo - Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for this Role

Hybrid 2-3 times/week: Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least twice a week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/

companySlogan: Challenging the status quo to create a new generation of financial services in Latin America. Join our revolution.
companyDescription:
Nu was born in 2013 with the mission to fight complexity to empower people in their daily lives by reinventing financial services.

We are one of the world’s largest digital banking platforms, serving more than 100 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. 

As one of the leading technology companies in the world, Nu leverages proprietary technologies and innovative business practices to create new financial solutions and experiences for individuals and SMEs that are simple, intuitive, convenient, low-cost, empowering, and human. 

Guided by its mission, Nu is fostering access to financial services across Latin America.

title: Senior IT Engineer – Global Offices Network
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 127 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America — and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we’re building.

Listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: NU), we combine proprietary technology, data intelligence, and an efficient operating model to deliver financial products that are simple, accessible, and human. Our impact has been recognized by global rankings such as Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies, and Forbes World’s Best Bank.

Learn more about our career opportunities here.

About The Role

We are looking for a Senior IT Engineer to design, implement, and operate our global office and data center network. In this role, you will own the connectivity and performance of Nu’s physical locations, ensuring that our offices and data centers are reliable, secure, and ready to support our next waves of growth.

You will work on routing, switching, wireless, security, and observability across our global stack, leading key initiatives such as new office rollouts, critical connectivity improvements, and the evolution towards Infrastructure as Code (IaC). You will be part of the Global Offices Network team, working closely with peers in Connectivity, Security, and Workplace to provide a seamless experience for Nubankers worldwide.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Network & Connectivity

  • Design, implement, and support enterprise network infrastructure for offices and data centers, including routing, switching, and wireless.
  • Maintain and evolve high‑availability data center fabrics (e.g., spine‑and‑leaf topologies with Juniper/Cisco hardware).
  • Ensure robust connectivity between offices and data centers, including WAN and SD‑WAN solutions, focusing on performance, reliability, and scalability.

Wireless, Load Balancing & Security

  • Configure and support wireless networks (controllers and access points), with strong experience in vendors such as Aruba and Cisco.
  • Operate and tune load balancers (e.g., F5, including DNS/ZoneRunner concepts) to guarantee optimal application delivery and resilience.
  • Collaborate with security teams on Next‑Generation Firewalls (e.g., Palo Alto, Fortinet) and cloud security brokers (e.g., Zscaler) in a “security‑first” network design.

Automation, IaC & Observability

  • Drive the transition away from manual configuration towards Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using tools such as GitHub, Terraform, and/or Ansible.
  • Implement and maintain network performance monitoring and observability using tools like PRTG, Prometheus, Grafana, SolarWinds or similar, ensuring proactive detection and resolution of incidents.
  • Manage PKI-related activities, including SSL/TLS certificate lifecycle and ensuring secure encrypted communications across services and endpoints.

Delivery & Collaboration

  • Lead or co‑lead new office implementations and major network changes, coordinating with Workplace, Connectivity, and other partner teams.
  • Act as a technical reference for incident response and problem management in critical networking issues, helping restore services quickly and preventing recurrences.
  • Document architectures, runbooks, and standards, helping scale knowledge across the team.
  • Mentor more junior engineers and collaborate in a multi‑level team (IC6, IC5, IC3, IC4) to continuously improve how we operate our global infrastructure.

We Are Looking for a Person Who Has

  • 5+ years of experience in enterprise networking or infrastructure engineering, working with critical environments (e.g., large offices, data centers, or mission‑critical operations).
  • Strong hands‑on experience with routing and switching, ideally using vendors such as Cisco and/or Juniper.
  • Experience implementing and supporting wireless networks, especially with Aruba and/or Cisco wireless solutions.
  • Solid experience operating network monitoring and performance tools (e.g., PRTG, SolarWinds, Prometheus, Grafana or similar) in production environments.
  • Experience with load balancers (e.g., F5) and DNS‑related components (e.g., ZoneRunner or equivalent).
  • Exposure to network security components, such as enterprise firewalls and/or security brokers, and comfort collaborating with security teams in a “security‑first” posture.
  • Experience with or strong interest in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and automation for network configuration and lifecycle management (e.g., GitHub, Terraform, Ansible).
  • Ability to lead initiatives and coordinate teams during new office rollouts, complex changes, and incident response.
  • Strong problem‑solving skills, ownership mindset, and ability to work in cross‑functional, multicultural teams.
  • Intermediate English (spoken and written), with the ability to participate in meetings and collaborate with global teams.

Nice to Have

  • Familiarity with Zero Trust network concepts and security‑driven networking.
  • Experience with cloud networking (e.g., connectivity to public clouds) and hybrid environments.
  • Experience with security mindset and encryption topics (e.g., secure protocols, network segmentation), even if not your primary specialization.
  • Prior experience in distributed or global organizations.

Location

This opportunity is based in Brazil, with a strong preference for candidates who can work from our offices in:

  • Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
  • Belo Horizonte, Brazil

You will be part of the Brazil Global Offices Network team and collaborate with colleagues in other locations as needed.

Our Benefits – Brazil

For Employees Based In Brazil, We Offer

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale-Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo – Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for This Role

This role follows our hybrid work model. Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least 2–3 times per week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit our article about Nu’s hybrid work model.

Learn More About How We Build Technology At Nu

descriptionText:
About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 127 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America — and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we’re building.

Listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: NU), we combine proprietary technology, data intelligence, and an efficient operating model to deliver financial products that are simple, accessible, and human. Our impact has been recognized by global rankings such as Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies, and Forbes World’s Best Bank.

Learn more about our career opportunities here.

About The Role

We are looking for a Senior IT Engineer to design, implement, and operate our global office and data center network. In this role, you will own the connectivity and performance of Nu’s physical locations, ensuring that our offices and data centers are reliable, secure, and ready to support our next waves of growth.

You will work on routing, switching, wireless, security, and observability across our global stack, leading key initiatives such as new office rollouts, critical connectivity improvements, and the evolution towards Infrastructure as Code (IaC). You will be part of the Global Offices Network team, working closely with peers in Connectivity, Security, and Workplace to provide a seamless experience for Nubankers worldwide.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Network & Connectivity

Design, implement, and support enterprise network infrastructure for offices and data centers, including routing, switching, and wireless.Maintain and evolve high‑availability data center fabrics (e.g., spine‑and‑leaf topologies with Juniper/Cisco hardware).Ensure robust connectivity between offices and data centers, including WAN and SD‑WAN solutions, focusing on performance, reliability, and scalability.

Wireless, Load Balancing & Security

Configure and support wireless networks (controllers and access points), with strong experience in vendors such as Aruba and Cisco.Operate and tune load balancers (e.g., F5, including DNS/ZoneRunner concepts) to guarantee optimal application delivery and resilience.Collaborate with security teams on Next‑Generation Firewalls (e.g., Palo Alto, Fortinet) and cloud security brokers (e.g., Zscaler) in a “security‑first” network design.

Automation, IaC & Observability

Drive the transition away from manual configuration towards Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using tools such as GitHub, Terraform, and/or Ansible.Implement and maintain network performance monitoring and observability using tools like PRTG, Prometheus, Grafana, SolarWinds or similar, ensuring proactive detection and resolution of incidents.Manage PKI-related activities, including SSL/TLS certificate lifecycle and ensuring secure encrypted communications across services and endpoints.

Delivery & Collaboration

Lead or co‑lead new office implementations and major network changes, coordinating with Workplace, Connectivity, and other partner teams.Act as a technical reference for incident response and problem management in critical networking issues, helping restore services quickly and preventing recurrences.Document architectures, runbooks, and standards, helping scale knowledge across the team.Mentor more junior engineers and collaborate in a multi‑level team (IC6, IC5, IC3, IC4) to continuously improve how we operate our global infrastructure.

We Are Looking for a Person Who Has

5+ years of experience in enterprise networking or infrastructure engineering, working with critical environments (e.g., large offices, data centers, or mission‑critical operations).Strong hands‑on experience with routing and switching, ideally using vendors such as Cisco and/or Juniper.Experience implementing and supporting wireless networks, especially with Aruba and/or Cisco wireless solutions.Solid experience operating network monitoring and performance tools (e.g., PRTG, SolarWinds, Prometheus, Grafana or similar) in production environments.Experience with load balancers (e.g., F5) and DNS‑related components (e.g., ZoneRunner or equivalent).Exposure to network security components, such as enterprise firewalls and/or security brokers, and comfort collaborating with security teams in a “security‑first” posture.Experience with or strong interest in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and automation for network configuration and lifecycle management (e.g., GitHub, Terraform, Ansible).Ability to lead initiatives and coordinate teams during new office rollouts, complex changes, and incident response.Strong problem‑solving skills, ownership mindset, and ability to work in cross‑functional, multicultural teams.Intermediate English (spoken and written), with the ability to participate in meetings and collaborate with global teams.

Nice to Have

Familiarity with Zero Trust network concepts and security‑driven networking.Experience with cloud networking (e.g., connectivity to public clouds) and hybrid environments.Experience with security mindset and encryption topics (e.g., secure protocols, network segmentation), even if not your primary specialization.Prior experience in distributed or global organizations.

Location

This opportunity is based in Brazil, with a strong preference for candidates who can work from our offices in:

Rio de Janeiro, BrazilBelo Horizonte, Brazil

You will be part of the Brazil Global Offices Network team and collaborate with colleagues in other locations as needed.

Our Benefits – Brazil

For Employees Based In Brazil, We Offer

Chance of earning equity at NubankFood/Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale-Alimentação)Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance ProgramLife InsuranceMedical PlanDental PlanNuLanguage – Language Course ProgramNucleo – Our learning platform of coursesExtended Parental LeaveDaycare AllowanceParental ConsultancyWork-from-home AllowanceGym Partnerships30 days of paid vacationRelocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for This Role

This role follows our hybrid work model. Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least 2–3 times per week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit our article about Nu’s hybrid work model.

Learn More About How We Build Technology At Nu

title: Senior Media BI Specialist
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 127 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we're building.

Listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: NU), we combine proprietary technology, data intelligence, and an efficient operating model to deliver financial products that are simple, accessible, and human.

Our impact has been recognized by global rankings such as Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies, and Forbes World’s Best Bank. Visit our institutional page Careers at Nu - Join our team!

About The Role

At Nu, paid media is one of our most strategic growth investments. As a Media BI Specialist, you’ll be responsible for measuring, analyzing, and explaining the real impact of those investments.

You’ll join the Media team as an analytics-first role, focused on performance measurement, incrementality, and ROI across the full funnel. Your work will connect paid media data with product usage and financial outcomes, helping define official metrics and guide investment decisions.

It’s a measurement and decision-support role, requiring strong analytical depth, ownership of data and dashboards, and the ability to turn complex analyses into clear recommendations for leadership.

Responsibilities

Full-Funnel Performance Analysis & Paid Media Measurement

  • Analyze awareness, consideration, and performance campaigns across paid media channels, connecting media metrics to real funnel and business outcomes.
  • Evaluate CAC, CPA, ROI, payback, marginal efficiency, and internal KPIs (e.g., BHT) by channel, campaign, audience, and time period.
  • Go beyond CTR and CPM to assess true value creation and long-term impact.
  • Build and analyze cross-sell and retention cohorts by channel and acquisition period.

Media × Product × Business Integration

  • Connect paid media performance with product activation, usage, and revenue generation.
  • Partner with product, growth, and finance teams to align marketing metrics with business outcomes.
  • Support investment decisions with clear, data-backed insights focused on value and incrementality.

Awareness & Consideration Measurement (Business-Oriented)

  • Measure the impact of top- and mid-funnel campaigns beyond standard media metrics (reach, CPM, CTR).
  • Apply internal methodologies, Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM), and incrementality frameworks to assess real business impact.
  • Challenge attribution models and assumptions when they fail to explain observed results.

Modeling, Forecasting & Media Planning

  • Develop models for:
  • Volume, ROI, and investment forecasting
  • Budget allocation and scenario simulations
  • Support monthly and quarterly media planning with data-driven recommendations.

Experimentation & Incrementality

  • Design, structure, and analyze A/B tests, holdouts, and geo experiments.
  • Measure the true incremental impact of campaigns, channels, and audiences.
  • Inform decisions to scale, optimize, or pause paid media investments.

Data Governance, Audiences & Reporting

  • Guarantee consistency, reliability, and trustworthiness of paid media and business data.
  • Own the definition of official media and marketing metrics, including internal KPIs such as BHT.
  • Be responsible for audience governance, including targeting audiences, control groups, test bases, and exclusions used across media platforms.
  • Define standards for dashboards and reporting, ensuring a single source of truth.
  • Produce executive-level reports with clear insights and recommendations for senior leadership.

Qualifications

Required

  • 5+ years of relevant experience in marketing analytics, growth analytics, media analytics, or similar roles.
  • Strong hands-on experience analyzing paid media performance and large-scale marketing investments.
  • Proven experience with incrementality, experimentation, and/or attribution models.
  • Advanced proficiency in SQL and BI tools (e.g., Looker, Power BI, Tableau).
  • Solid experience with cohort analysis, LTV, CAC, ROI, and funnel metrics.
  • Strong foundation in applied statistics and experimental design.
  • Ability to translate complex analyses into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders.
  • Business-oriented mindset with a critical approach to metrics and hypotheses.
  • Proficiency in English.

Nice to Have

  • Experience working closely with paid media platforms such as Google, Meta, or similar ecosystems.
  • Background in fintech, digital products, or data-driven growth environments.
  • Experience influencing budget allocation and investment decisions at scale.

Our Benefits

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/ Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo - Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for this Role

Hybrid 1week/cycle: Our hybrid work model brings us to the office for one week every cycle, which may vary between one and three months. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/

Explore How We Build Technology At Nubank

🔗 building.nubank.com.br ↗

🎥 youtube.com/@building.nubank ↗

🎧 Listen to our stories on Spotify ↗

title: Pessoa Business Analyst - exclusiva para Pessoas com Deficiência
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 122 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we're building.

Listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: NU), we combine proprietary technology, data intelligence, and an efficient operating model to deliver financial products that are simple, accessible, and human.

Our impact has been recognized by global rankings such as Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies, and Forbes World’s Best Bank. Visit our institutional page https://international.nubank.com.br/careers/

Business Analysts at Nubank

As a company at the forefront of the financial revolution in Brazil, Nubank has experienced exponential growth since its inception. To keep your growth path healthy, numerous critical decisions constantly need to be made. Business Analysts are fundamental in the decision process because they must not only be able to deal with large amounts of information and data, but also always think of ways to improve our clients' experience.

It is BA's responsibility to structure the data, analyze it and use the results to test different solutions with the aim of improving Nubank's efficiency.

We are looking for people who are independent and who are not afraid to take the initiative to identify and solve problems that are not necessarily obvious.

You can read more about it in our blog and get some insights into how we work.

As a Business Analyst, You’re Expected To

  • Do a lot of investigative work, focused on deciding which data makes the most sense to use in which situation. If you don't have the data, it's your job to think about what tests can be done or how to extract the information you need to answer your questions;
  • Support your team in making data-based decisions by monitoring analysis and decisions, ensuring the flow of projects and making the process of taking risks comfortable despite the uncertainties;
  • Learning how to work with a large volume of data to build a solid foundation for decision making with calculated risks - even knowing that not all the answers are in the data and/or that not all the data you would like is available;Use tools like SQL, Python and Scala - taught during your first weeks with us - for data extraction;
  • Understand our products and interactions with other areas;
  • Take advantage of opportunities to grow and learn from a well-prepared and highly qualified team;
  • Opportunity to work in a complex, dynamic and diverse environment, participating in projects that will impact a large number of clients.

What are we looking for?

  • People with disabilities (PcD - pessoa com deficiencia)
  • Analytical capacity: ease with numbers and calculations, having the ability to solve problems mathematically;
  • Business basics: dynamics between risks and opportunities, as well as interaction between business areas and technical areas (products/engineering);
  • Problem solving: ease and creativity to seek solutions to day-to-day challenges and achieve the best results;
  • Ability to understand how your decisions and work impact our customers' experience, seeking results that align with our purpose;
  • Willingness and ease of learning, especially for tools;
  • Openness to receive feedback and incorporate it into your daily life and development;
  • Adaptability to the multifunctional environment;
  • Good english speaking skills to be a part of a global team;
  • Profiles that add value to Nubank's culture.

Our Benefits

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/ Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo - Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for this Role

Hybrid 2-3 times/week: Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least twice a week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/

title: Technical Lead Product Designer - Crypto
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 122 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we're building.

Listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: NU), we combine proprietary technology, data intelligence, and an efficient operating model to deliver financial products that are simple, accessible, and human.

Our impact has been recognized by global rankings such as Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies, and Forbes World’s Best Bank. Visit our institutional page https://international.nubank.com.br/careers/

At Nu, We're Reimagining How Crypto Can Be Better.

Crypto has a complexity problem. For most people, it's still intimidating, opaque, and far from the effortless experience it could be. At Nu, we believe that's a design problem—and we're looking for someone to help us solve it.

We're hiring a Lead Product Designer to join our growing crypto team. You'll be helping shape experiences that make crypto genuinely accessible.

Once Here, You Will

  • Be a technical reference for the team, seeking to adapt and replicate the best practices in the market, increasing the team’s bar;
  • Build links with other business areas at all levels of the organization, influencing business decisions advocating for user-centered design;
  • Inspire fellow product designers, by creating a high bar of quality design work;
  • Establish and promote a healthy team culture that works hand in hand with cross-functional partners and design teams in other Nubank offices;
  • Push the boundaries of your team’s UX/UI/visual execution and help Nubank challenge the status quo.

Requirements

  • Solid expertise in Product Design (UI, UX, and Research);
  • Experience in digital products;
  • Excellent English: verbal and written communication skills;
  • Ability to work well in a highly collaborative and cross-functional team;
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic startup environment;
  • Ability to influence prioritization in an agile environment. Adapt to shifting priorities and evolving stakeholder’s needs with openness and agility.

Benefits

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/ Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo - Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for this Role

Hybrid 2-3 times/week: Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least twice a week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/

As one of the leading technology companies in the world, Nu leverages proprietary technologies and innovative business practices to create new financial solutions and experiences for individuals and SMEs that are simple, intuitive, convenient, low-cost, empowering, and human. 

Guided by its mission, Nu is fostering access to financial services across Latin America.

title: Lead IT Engineer - Service Management
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 127 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America — and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we're building. Listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: NU), we combine proprietary technology, data intelligence, and an efficient operating model to deliver financial products that are simple, accessible, and human. Our impact has been recognized by global rankings such as Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies, and Forbes World’s Best Bank. Learn more at https://international.nubank.com.br/careers/.

About The Team

The IT team is the operational backbone of Nubank’s internal infrastructure. As the company scales, we’re building the service management foundations that let engineering teams move faster with more confidence — turning IT operations into a strategic advantage, not just a support function.

About The Role

As a Lead IT Engineer - Service Management, you will own and evolve Nubank’s IT Service Management (ITSM) practice end to end. You’ll design and implement processes, tools, and governance that keep our internal services reliable, auditable, and efficient, enabling engineering teams to focus on high-impact work while maintaining strong operational controls in a regulated environment.

You’ll Be Responsible For

  • Continuing to build and own Nubank's ITSM practice, defining and implementing core ITIL v4 processes: incident, problem, change, service request, and knowledge management.
  • Establishing a service catalog with clearly defined SLAs and OLAs aligned with internal stakeholder expectations.
  • Owning the end-to-end ITSM toolchain (Jira Service Management or equivalent): configuration, workflow design, form logic, SLA rules, and integrations with adjacent systems.
  • Identifying and closing automation gaps — routing, escalation, notifications, and reporting — partnering with engineering teams to reduce manual toil across operations.
  • Defining and tracking operational KPIs (MTTR, SLA compliance, first-contact resolution, change success rate) and building regular reporting cadences for leadership and stakeholders.
  • Establishing a Change Advisory Board (CAB) with structured documentation and approval workflows.
  • Supporting audit and compliance requirements with structured evidence of IT operational controls, essential in a regulated financial environment.
  • Driving ITSM adoption across the organization, creating runbooks, training materials, and documentation that embed service management culture at scale.

We Are Looking For a Person Who Has

Mandatory Qualifications

  • 7+ years of experience in IT service management, IT operations, or a related discipline.
  • Deep ITIL v4 expertise; ITIL 4 Foundation or Managing Professional certification strongly preferred.
  • Proven track record of building ITSM practices from scratch or significantly transforming an existing function.
  • Hands-on experience owning and configuring ITSM tooling end-to-end (Jira Service Management, ServiceNow, or equivalent) — not just using it, but designing it.
  • Ability to work cross-functionally in a fast-paced, engineering-led environment, with strong influence-without-authority skills.
  • Strong written and verbal communication, including the ability to present to senior leadership.

Nice-to-have Qualifications

  • Experience in fintech, high-growth tech, or other regulated industries.
  • Familiarity with adjacent frameworks: COBIT, ISO 20000, or SRE principles.
  • Experience partnering with Security and/or Platform Engineering teams.
  • Fluent English.

What We Offer

  • Greenfield, high-visibility scope: you’ll define what “good” ITSM looks like at Nubank’s scale — from zero.
  • Direct exposure to senior leadership across IT and Engineering.
  • A culture that values ownership and getting foundations right, without bureaucracy for its own sake.

Our Benefits – Brazil

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo – Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

(For hybrid roles, commuting-related benefits such as Vale-Transporte and gym partnerships remain applicable.)

Work Model for This Role

Hybrid – 2–3 times per week in the office.

Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least twice a week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/.

Explore How We Build Technology At Nubank

  • building.nubank.com.br
  • youtube.com/@building.nubank
  • Our stories on Spotify

Diversity & Inclusion

At Nubank, we want to be sure that we’re building a more diverse and inclusive workplace that reflects the customers we serve and seek to empower. That’s why we hire based on equality. We consider gender, ethnicity, race, religion, sexual orientation, and other identity markers as enriching elements to our company, while ensuring none of them represent a barrier when recruiting fantastic talent.

How to Apply

If you’re excited about building a robust IT Service Management foundation that enables Nubank to scale with reliability and speed, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now and help us shape the future of financial services in Latin America.

title: Especialista Back Office Financeiro
descriptionHtml:
Sobre o Nu

Nu é uma das maiores plataformas digitais de serviços financeiros do mundo, com mais de 127 milhões de clientes no Brasil, México e Colômbia. Guiados pela missão de combater a complexidade e empoderar pessoas, estamos redefinindo os serviços financeiros na América Latina – e isso ainda é só o começo do futuro roxo que estamos construindo.

Listados na Bolsa de Nova York (NYSE: NU), combinamos tecnologia proprietária, inteligência de dados e um modelo operacional eficiente para oferecer produtos financeiros simples, acessíveis e humanos. Nosso impacto já foi reconhecido por rankings como Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies e Forbes World’s Best Bank. Saiba mais em https://international.nubank.com.br/careers/



Sobre a vaga

No Nu, o time de Back Office é fundamental para garantir que toda a engrenagem operacional por trás dos nossos produtos funcione com precisão, segurança e escala.

Como Especialista Back Office Financeiro, você será responsável por assegurar a integridade operacional das rotinas de liquidação, reconciliação e tratamento de exceções, garantindo que cada movimentação financeira esteja correta, rastreável e em conformidade com nossos controles internos e regulamentações aplicáveis. Seu trabalho impacta diretamente a experiência de clientes, a eficiência operacional e a confiabilidade das nossas informações financeiras.

No dia a dia, você vai

  • Operacionalização e acompanhamento do fluxo de Bloqueio Judicial e transferência de ativos.
  • Participar do processo de liquidação e movimentações dos clientes da Corretora.
  • Acompanhamento e operacionalização de pedidos de Bloqueio Judicial em contas de clientes.
  • Garantir que as movimentações financeiras nas contas dos clientes estejam conciliadas.
  • Garantir que as operações e financeiro dos clientes estejam corretamente refletidas nos sistemas.
  • Auxiliar na contínua definição de melhorias nos processos e sistemas relacionados à backoffice financeiro.

Buscamos uma pessoa que tenha

  • Experiência prévia em Back Office / Operações em instituições financeiras, meios de pagamento, adquirentes, bancos digitais ou fintechs.
  • Vivência com reconciliação de transações financeiras (ex.: cartões, TED/Pix, boletos, carteiras digitais, cobranças) e tratamento de exceções.
  • Domínio de Excel/Google Sheets (tabelas dinâmicas, fórmulas, filtros avançados) para análise e reconciliação.
  • Comunicação clara e estruturada, com capacidade de traduzir temas operacionais complexos para diferentes áreas de negócio.
  • Certificação PQO backoffice - ANBIMA módulos I e II

Diferenciais

  • Experiência prévia em fintechs, bancos digitais ou ambientes de alto crescimento e transformação.
  • Vivência com SQL, PYTHON/VBA/RPA ou ferramentas de consulta a dados para apoiar análises e reconciliações.
  • Inglês para interação com times ou stakeholders globais.

Localização

  • São Paulo, Brasil (modelo de trabalho híbrido).

Nossos benefícios – Brasil (CLT)

Como pessoa Back Office Specialist contratada como CLT no Brasil, você contará com:

  • Chance de receber equity no Nubank
  • Vale-Refeição e/ou Vale-Alimentação
  • Vale-Transporte
  • NuCare – programa de assistência psicológica, financeira e jurídica
  • Seguro de Vida
  • Plano Médico
  • Plano Odontológico
  • NuLanguage – programa de cursos de idiomas
  • Nucleo – nossa plataforma de cursos e aprendizagem
  • Licença Parental Estendida
  • Auxílio Creche
  • Consultoria Parental
  • Auxílio para trabalho remoto (Work-from-home Allowance)
  • Parcerias com academias
  • 30 dias de férias remuneradas
  • Pacote de Recolocação, se aplicável

Modelo de trabalho para esta vaga

  • Híbrido 2–3x por semana no escritório de São Paulo: nosso modelo híbrido leva as pessoas ao escritório pelo menos duas vezes por semana, em dias estratégicos para maximizar conexão e colaboração do time. Para mais detalhes, acesse: https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/

Explore como construímos tecnologia (e o futuro dos serviços financeiros) no Nu: building.nubank.com.br, youtube.com/@building.nubank e nosso podcast no Spotify.



Candidate-se

Se você quer fazer parte da construção do futuro dos serviços financeiros, garantindo uma operação robusta, precisa e escalável por trás dos nossos produtos, candidate-se e venha ser Back Office Specialist no Nu em São Paulo.

title: Machine Learning Engineer Manager (AI Platform)
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 127 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we're building.

About The Role

AI Platform Team - The mission of the AI Platform team is bold: to democratize AI by empowering every Nubanker—not just Data Scientists—with safe, scalable, and accurate Machine Learning capabilities.

We are looking for a Manager who is passionate about creating solutions that push the boundaries of our current stack. You will help us move beyond basic DS work to abstract the complexities of the entire ML lifecycle—from training and deployment to serving and governance.

Why is this role open?

We are scaling. Currently, our structure has grown significantly, and to ensure the organization's health and the growth of our engineers, we are opening this position to lead a dedicated squad. You will take ownership of a team that manages the base infrastructure for the entire Data Science function at Nubank.

You'll Be Responsible For

As the ML Manager for the AI Platform, you will combine technical leadership with people management. You will lead a squad of 11 Machine Learning Engineers (varying from Senior to Intern levels) and work closely with a Staff ML Engineer and a Product Manager.

  • People Management: Foster a culture of feedback and growth. You will guide the development of your direct reports, conducting performance reviews and helping them navigate their careers.
  • Strategic Roadmap: Translate high-level business goals into actionable technical roadmaps. You will discuss needs with stakeholders and define the "how" and "when."
  • Architecture & Innovation: Explore architecture gaps and opportunities. You won't just maintain; you will challenge the team to explore complex features like scalable parallel processing and better UX for Data Scientists.
  • Execution: Conduct initiatives with autonomy and diligence, ensuring we deliver value while maintaining high engineering standards.

Nubank already has well-established tools for basic Data Science work. Your challenge is to take us to the next level. You will push the frontiers of technology to handle massive scale, integrate with multiple complex systems, and democratize access to AI tools across the company.

What We Are Looking For

You don't need to be a hands-on coder 100% of the time, but you must have the technical depth to challenge the team and understand the ecosystem.

  • Experience in Leadership: You have experience managing high-performing engineering teams, sharing feedback, and hiring/developing talent.
  • Domain Expertise: Solid background in ML Engineering, AI, or Data Science, specifically with a focus on MLOps, Infrastructure, and Platform building.
  • Tech Stack Familiarity: You are comfortable discussing and architecting systems involving Kubeflow, SageMaker, and Databricks.
  • The "Builder" Mindset: You enjoy building software. You want autonomy to make decisions and explore edge technologies, similar to the engineering culture found in Big Tech

Our Benefits

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/ Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo - Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for this Role

Hybrid 2-3 times/week: Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least twice a week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/

title: Lead Machine Learning Engineer / Applied AI Scientist
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Nu was born in 2013 with the mission to fight complexity to empower people in their daily lives by reinventing financial services. We are one of the world’s largest digital banking platforms, serving millions of customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia.

About The Role

At AI Core, we are scaling the impact of our AI initiatives to become the primary driver of Nubank’s most critical decision systems. We are seeking an Lead Machine Learning Engineer (Applied AI Scientist) to lead high-impact research projects that bridge the gap between state-of-the-art AI and production-grade financial systems. You will be responsible for solving complex, ambiguous problems using Deep Learning and Foundation Models, ensuring our architectures are scalable, efficient, and driving measurable business results.

As an Applied AI Scientist (MLE), you’re expected to:

  • Research Execution & Technical Leadership (Complexity & Autonomy)
  • Lead and execute complex applied research initiatives independently, focusing on building and optimizing architectures (e.g., Transformers, GNNs) that can be deployed across critical use cases like Credit, RecSys, GenAI, and real-time inference.
  • Address difficult and ambiguous modeling problems that require coordination across various stakeholders (Data, Infra, Product), delivering innovative solutions with a clear focus on medium-term impact.
  • Bridge the gap between research and production by designing architectures that respect MLOps constraints, ensuring models are optimized for latency, interpretability, and cost-efficiency.
  • Strategic Impact & Collaboration (Impact)
  • Develop and deliver innovative solutions that address project-level challenges, focusing on pushing the latest platform and AI research improvements into downstream production models.
  • Actively participate in cross-functional collaborations, ensuring that research outputs are seamlessly integrated into Nubank's decision-making engines.
  • Establish technical standards within the AI Core team for experimentation, model evaluation, and code quality, inspiring peers to raise their performance.
  • Mentorship & Function Contribution (Function Contribution)
  • Serve as a technical mentor for senior engineers and researchers, providing guidance on deep learning fundamentals, problem formulation, and research methodology.
  • Actively contribute to the function's growth by participating in mandatory activities like hiring (interview panels) and leading internal task forces to improve our ML lifecycle.
  • Contribute to thought leadership by participating in research collaborations or internal papers that align with Nubank’s strategic goals.

What are we looking for?

  • Professional Experience: 5-7+ years in applied AI/ML, with a proven track record of delivering research-driven systems into production environments
  • Technical Mastery:
    • Deep expertise in Deep Learning architectures (Transformers, Multimodal, or GNNs).
    • Strong coding skills in Python and proficiency with frameworks like PyTorch, JAX, or TensorFlow.
    • Solid understanding of MLOps and the constraints of deploying models at scale.
  • Problem Solving: Sophisticated skills in ML problem formulation and the ability to navigate uncertainty when data is messy or unavailable.
  • Communication: Ability to communicate complex technical concepts to both technical peers and cross-functional stakeholders, ensuring alignment and buy-in.
  • Analytical Capacity: Experience with large-scale experimentation and A/B testing to validate research hypotheses.
Our Benefits

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/ Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo - Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for this Role

Hybrid 2-3 times/week: Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least twice a week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/

title: Model Risk Senior Specialist
descriptionHtml:
About Nubank

Nubank was founded in 2013 to free people from a bureaucratic, slow, and inefficient financial system. Since then, through innovative technology and outstanding customer service, the company has been redefining people's relationships with money across Latin America. With operations in Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia, Nubank is one of the world’s largest digital banking platforms and technology-leading companies.

Today, Nubank is a global company with offices in São Paulo (Brazil), Mexico City (Mexico), Buenos Aires (Argentina), Bogotá (Colombia), Durham (United States), and Berlin (Germany). It was founded in 2013 in Sao Paulo by Colombian David Vélez, and cofounded by Brazilian Cristina Junqueira and American Edward Wible. For more information, visit www.nubank.com.br

About The Team

At Nubank we heavily rely on data, machine learning, and AI to drive our strategy and provide the best experience and products to our customers.The Model Risk team plays a crucial role in ensuring Nubank relies on world-class models that meet business and customer needs, and have associated risks under control. We act by providing effective review & challenge and identifying opportunities to improve our models. We also act by implementing governance and validation standards to control the risk of our models, as well as by establishing feedback loops to constantly improve them.

The Position

This is a senior individual contributor role that will require experience in machine learning and analytics, along with knowledge of Expected Credit Loss and its parameters (PD, EAD, LGD).

You will be responsible for

  • Conduct independent reviews of quantitative models based on financial, statistical, and machine learning techniques, with a focus on credit risk provisions and capital
  • Provide effective challenge to models and modelling processes, identify risks and engage with other teams to strengthen our decision-making tools and capabilities.
  • Develop playbooks and toolkits (e.g., Python, SQL, Scala) to optimize model reviews, ongoing monitoring, and assess the impact of models on decision-making.
  • Contribute to the construction of Nubank's Model Risk Management and Model Review processes with autonomy and creativity.
  • Discuss and report model risk status and receive independent opinions from various stakeholders, including senior managers and regulators.
  • Be exposed to models from different countries and regulatory requirements.
  • Have a strong understanding of relevant local and international regulations and accounting standards concerning credit provisions, including BACEN 4966 and IFRS9.
  • Ensure the team maintains technical excellence and actively contributes to establishing model risk controls and processes that strengthen compliance with regulations.
  • Mentor junior staff on best practices for Model Oversight and Validation.
  • Work in a multicultural, diverse and highly skilled environment.

We Are Looking For a Person Who Has

  • Experience developing or validating quantitative and/or machine learning models, with a focus on credit provisions and capital requirements models.
  • Interest in reviewing complex machine learning and other types of models to identify risks and propose enhancements.
  • Data Science skills, strong knowledge of machine learning tools and techniques.
  • Strong programming skills, desirable previous experience with Python, SQL, GitHub.
  • Ability to learn fast, strong analytical, and problem-solving skills.
  • Organized and detail-oriented, without losing track of the big picture.
  • English language proficiency.
  • Experience in relevant local and international regulations and accounting standards related to credit provisions and capital, such as BACEN 4966, IFRS9, ICAAP.

Benefits

  • Health, dental, and life insurance
  • Meal allowance
  • Transportation assistance
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Parking partnership - discounted parking in our office
  • Free bike parking with showers available
  • NuCare - Our mental health and wellness assistance program
  • NuLanguage - Our language learning program
  • Gympass partnership
  • Extended maternity and paternity Leaves
  • Child care allowance
  • ‘Espaço Feijão’- Private nursing and breastfeeding spaces in our buildings
  • Onsite Health Center - Medical support for every Nubanker in our office

title: Senior Analytics Engineer
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 127 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we're building.

Listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: NU), we combine proprietary technology, data intelligence, and an efficient operating model to deliver financial products that are simple, accessible, and human.

Our impact has been recognized by global rankings such as Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies, and Forbes World’s Best Bank. Visit our institutional page https://international.nubank.com.br/careers/

About The Role

As Analytics Engineers at Nubank, we are the bridge between the business needs and the data platform. We make data available to the entire company, always thinking about the best modeling, governance and performance. In addition, we share best practices and create new tools so that everyone who consumes the data can work in a more organized, efficient and secure way.

To help you better understand the role, we’ve included a few helpful links below:

Data Analysts are now called Analytics Engineers

Nubank is looking for a bold and impact-driven Analytics Engineer to join our Data Platform team. This is a full-time opportunity focused on designing, building and scaling data solutions that enable better decision-making across the company. Our Analytics Engineer team operates at scale, supporting multiple business domains with reliable, high-quality and well-governed data products. The role sits at the intersection of data architecture, engineering excellence and business impact, with high autonomy to lead end-to-end data initiatives and influence our data strategy.

You'll be responsible for

End-to-End Data Projects: leading data projects with high autonomy, from requirements gathering and stakeholder alignment to database modeling and data delivery.

Data Pipelines & Ingestion: designing, building and maintaining robust data pipelines, sometimes from scratch, ensuring reliability, scalability and performance.

Data Platform Engineering: contributing to the design, documentation, maintenance, monitoring and optimization of our data codebase and tools.

ETL/ELT Optimization: identifying opportunities to improve scalability, performance and reliability across our ETL/ELT platforms.

Business Translation: translating business needs into data products and solutions aligned with Nubank’s data platform tools, architecture and long-term strategy.

Stakeholder Partnership: negotiating and defining data strategies with technical and business stakeholders to deliver high-impact solutions, always aligned with Nubank’s data governance principles.

Mentorship & Data Literacy: acting as a mentor to team members and contributing to data literacy initiatives across Nubank.

We're Looking For Someone Who Has

  • Solid experience in Data Architecture & Design, working in complex and/or large-scale data environments.
  • Strong ability to write highly optimized SQL queries to solve complex problems and perform data modeling.
  • Hands-on experience implementing and maintaining data pipelines from scratch.
  • Strong programming experience with general-purpose languages (e.g. Python, Scala, R).
  • Passion for data and building high-quality data products.
  • Excellent communication skills to work effectively with both technical and business stakeholders.

Nice to Have

  • Knowledge of software engineering best practices (documentation, automated tests, clean code, etc.).
  • Experience with analytical data architectures and modeling techniques (e.g. Data Lake, Data Warehouse, Data Mart).
  • Familiarity with distributed data systems and parallel processing.
  • Ability to abstract business concepts into quantitative metrics (e.g. KPIs, OKRs).
  • Experience with data visualization tools such as Looker, Power BI, Tableau or similar.
  • Advanced English (B2+ according to CEFR).

Our Benefits

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/ Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo - Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for this Role

Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least twice a week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/



Explore How We Build Technology At Nubank

🔗 building.nubank.com.br ↗

🎥 youtube.com/@building.nubank ↗

🎧 Listen to our stories on Spotify ↗

title: Enterprise Risk Management
descriptionHtml:
About Us

Nu is one of the largest digital financial platforms in the world, with more than 122 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. Guided by our mission to fight complexity and empower people, we are redefining financial services in Latin America and this is still just the beginning of the purple future we're building.

Listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: NU), we combine proprietary technology, data intelligence, and an efficient operating model to deliver financial products that are simple, accessible, and human.

Our impact has been recognized by global rankings such as Time 100 Companies, Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies, and Forbes World’s Best Bank. Visit our institutional page https://international.nubank.com.br/careers/

About The Team

The Enterprise Risk Management squad sets the global standards for how Nubank identifies, assesses and manages risk across the enterprise – spanning operational, compliance, technology, conduct and other non‑financial risks, and strongly interfacing with financial risk areas (credit, market, liquidity, capital, treasury).

As part of the ERM Methodology Team, you will sit at the intersection of risk, product and platform: shaping global frameworks, embedding them into data and systems, and scaling them with AI‑enabled workflows so that ERM becomes both a competitive advantage and an enabler of safe growth across products, portfolios and geographies. You will be a reference for methodology decisions, influencing senior stakeholders across multiple lines of defense and risk types.

What You'll Do

  • Own the end‑to‑end design and evolution of ERM methodologies, ensuring coherence across programs and geographies.
  • Ensure ERM methodologies connect across risk types (e.g., operational, compliance, technology, conduct, model, financial/credit where relevant) and support a consistent view of the risk profile.
  • Translate methodological requirements into GRC platform, product and data specifications, partnering with Platform and engineering teams to build scalable, intuitive workflows.
  • Lead the global standardization of global ERM methodologies while enabling smart local adaptations to meet regulatory expectations in each geo.
  • Design and drive the integration of ERM programs in new geos, ensuring that new launches start already aligned with group‑level risk standards and governance.
  • Define and embed AI/agent use‑cases into ERM workflows (e.g., risk/control suggestions, narrative pre‑population, quality checks), with clear human‑in‑the‑loop governance.
  • Use AI and analytics to monitor quality and consistency of methodologies, surfacing insights and opportunities for continuous improvement.
  • Partner with risk leaders (financial and non-financial risks), CRO teams, risk stewards and 1LOD to ensure strong adoption and practical usability of ERM frameworks in day‑to‑day decision making.
  • Represent ERM methodologies in governance and decision forums.
  • Create and refine key risk and performance indicators for ERM programs, enabling better visibility on program effectiveness, maturity and trends.
  • Communicate clearly and regularly about ERM methodology initiatives and projects with a wide variety of stakeholders (risk teams in different countries, financial risk functions, internal audit, leadership, product and platform).

What We're Looking For (Minimum Qualifications)

  • Solid experience in Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk / Non‑Financial Risk, with hands‑on ownership of at least one major program or framework (RCSA, control testing, Issue Management, NP&F or equivalent enterprise‑level framework).
  • Proven track record as a senior individual contributor leading complex risk initiatives end‑to‑end, from design to rollout, with limited oversight.
  • Experience working in multi‑geo or global environments, balancing global standards with local regulatory expectations.
  • Strong ability to translate risk methodologies into systems and data (platform requirements, data models, workflow design) in partnership with engineering and product.
  • Demonstrated stakeholder management and influence without formal authority, especially across product, risk, compliance and platform/engineering teams.
  • Comfort working with data and analytics (e.g., querying datasets, interpreting dashboards, defining metrics) to guide decisions on methodology and program performance.
  • Experience defining and tracking metrics, SLAs and KPIs for risk/controls or governance processes.
  • Strong written and verbal communication in English, including the ability to explain complex risk concepts to non‑experts.

Nice to Have

  • Prior experience in ERM, operational risk, internal controls in financial services or fintech.
  • Previous work in designing frameworks, methodologies and governance for risk (financial and/or non‑financial), influencing multiple lines of defense and senior stakeholders across geos.
  • Exposure to AI/ML or advanced analytics applied to risk, controls, data quality or governance (e.g., LLMs, anomaly detection, smart suggestions).
  • Experience working closely with platform / GRC / risk systems (e.g., JIRA‑based workflows, custom risk platforms, governance tooling).
  • Familiarity with regulatory expectations for risks and governance in Brazil and/or other key markets, including reading requirements and translating them into practical frameworks.
  • Background in high‑growth tech/fintech environments, navigating the tension between speed, innovation and robust risk management.

Benefits

  • Chance of earning equity at Nubank
  • Food/ Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação)
  • Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte)
  • NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program
  • Life Insurance
  • Medical Plan
  • Dental Plan
  • NuLanguage – Language Course Program
  • Nucleo - Our learning platform of courses
  • Extended Parental Leave
  • Daycare Allowance
  • Parental Consultancy
  • Work-from-home Allowance
  • Gym Partnerships
  • 30 days of paid vacation
  • Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable

Work Model for this Role

Hybrid 2-3 times/week: Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least twice a week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/

Communicate clearly and regularly about ERM methodology initiatives and projects with a wide variety of stakeholders (risk teams in different countries, financial risk functions, internal audit, leadership, product and platform). 

What We're Looking For (Minimum Qualifications)

Solid experience in Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk / Non‑Financial Risk, with hands‑on ownership of at least one major program or framework (RCSA, control testing, Issue Management, NP&F or equivalent enterprise‑level framework). Proven track record as a senior individual contributor leading complex risk initiatives end‑to‑end, from design to rollout, with limited oversight. Experience working in multi‑geo or global environments, balancing global standards with local regulatory expectations. Strong ability to translate risk methodologies into systems and data (platform requirements, data models, workflow design) in partnership with engineering and product. Demonstrated stakeholder management and influence without formal authority, especially across product, risk, compliance and platform/engineering teams. Comfort working with data and analytics (e.g., querying datasets, interpreting dashboards, defining metrics) to guide decisions on methodology and program performance. Experience defining and tracking metrics, SLAs and KPIs for risk/controls or governance processes. Strong written and verbal communication in English, including the ability to explain complex risk concepts to non‑experts. 

Nice to Have

Prior experience in ERM, operational risk, internal controls in financial services or fintech. Previous work in designing frameworks, methodologies and governance for risk (financial and/or non‑financial), influencing multiple lines of defense and senior stakeholders across geos. Exposure to AI/ML or advanced analytics applied to risk, controls, data quality or governance (e.g., LLMs, anomaly detection, smart suggestions). Experience working closely with platform / GRC / risk systems (e.g., JIRA‑based workflows, custom risk platforms, governance tooling). Familiarity with regulatory expectations for risks and governance in Brazil and/or other key markets, including reading requirements and translating them into practical frameworks. Background in high‑growth tech/fintech environments, navigating the tension between speed, innovation and robust risk management. 

Benefits

Chance of earning equity at Nubank Food/ Meal Card (Vale-Refeição and/or Vale Alimentação) Public Transportation Commuting Benefit (Vale-Transporte) NuCare – Psychological, Financial and Legal Assistance Program Life Insurance Medical Plan Dental Plan NuLanguage – Language Course Program Nucleo - Our learning platform of courses Extended Parental Leave Daycare Allowance Parental Consultancy Work-from-home Allowance Gym Partnerships 30 days of paid vacation Relocation Assistance Package, if applicable 

Work Model for this Role

Hybrid 2-3 times/week: Our hybrid work model brings us to the office at least twice a week, on strategic days designed to maximize team connection and collaboration. For more details, visit https://building.nubank.com/nu-hybrid-work-model/
